sections.paragraph:
λῆμμα, ατος, τό (s. λαμβάνω and next entry; Soph., Pla. et al.; pap, LXX, Philo; Jos., Bell. 1, 289, Ant. 14, 393) someth. received gain διὰ τ. ἐπιθυμίαν ποῦ λ. because of the desire for gain Hs 9, 19, 3.—DELG s.v. λαμβάνω B.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 593
